Room experience, not convince for business travelerdue to dim room light, hard to ready documents and doing paperwork. Shower head problem,  some wall paper already peel off and makes room looks old and unmaintained. 
Breakfast, not much comment. Simple dish and not much choice.

reviews.title: HORRENDOUS budget room far from the city
reviews.text:
My experience was nothing less than aweful. 
I got a courtroom without window, on the 3rd floor, in a section I believe to be extended from the main wing.  
The route from elevator to my room was like a maze, routes within hotel confusing, haphazard floor plan, renovating materials and garbage chucked along the pathway, service store and security room side by side guest room, aroma oil air freshener lying along the corridor. hideous decorations. walls outside my room were not even painted! gruesome! Horrible security anyone can access the hotel rooms from the carpark and shopping mall elevators.
Tiles in the rooms are cracked, dont try walking around barefeet. Wall papers are peeling. Foul stench of tabacco fills the room. Leaking taps and leaking basin.   
For this kind of price, i am sure i can get better hotels elsewhere. If you are on budget, even the backpackers lodge could provide better comfort.
reviews.positives: Good cafe on top floor overlooking the mountains

reviews.title: Hidden fees and disappointing stay
reviews.text:
For rm135/night, this hotel was a letdown. The biggest issue was the parking: at check-in, staff said the fee was a one-time charge per entry. At checkout, they suddenly charged us per night—a misleading and costly surprise.

Additionally, early check-in was denied despite empty rooms. The A/C didn’t cool properly, the room was gloomy, and the bed was old and uncomfortable.

With hidden costs, poor communication, and dated facilities, this place offers terrible value. Won’t be returning.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text:

1. The staff were not very friendly, and they didn't provide detailed information upfront. We had to ask for basic details, and only then did we receive a response
2. There was insufficient parking space, and despite booking in advance, there was no confirmation regarding parking availability
3. The bed was unstable and kept shaking, which made it uncomfortable. Additionally, the iron provided ruined my shirt.
4. The room was very small, even though we booked for two adults and one child. It was not adequate for our needs.
5. The breakfast was disappointing. There was little variety, and the only dish that tasted decent was the nasi lemak. The roti canai was instant and lacked quality
Overall, I was dissatisfied with my stay and would not recommend this hotel based on this experience.
reviews.response:

We hope to have the opportunity to welcome you again and provide you with a better stay.

Warm regards,
Courtyard Hotel Management

Not being helpful, I just went to my room, and it took me more than 10 minutes to find my room, as the number of the room did not follow sequence, as it was jumped over. I stayed in room 360. When I reach the corner of the building, I only saw room 358, 359 and 361. I wondered, where is room 360. Eventually I found it after wandering for about 10 minutes, the room I located in the middle section of the building. Went to my room, wanted to make some coffee before dinner, and to my surprise, there is no coffee facilities provided, and I have to pay RM5 for a hot water jug. My overall experience in this hotel, was below expectation. And, I have no choice, as the week I am in this hotel, most of the hotel (which I experienced a very good service) are fully occupied.

reviews.title: Terrible hotel.
reviews.text: We stayed there for 3 nights. Initially we took 2 rooms but due to room space are so small we had no choice to take another room and we need to wait until 12am just to get the room. Security is bad, any random people can take the front lobby lift or the other lifts which attached to the 1 Borneo shopping mall or even from the mall's car park. Those random people can easily have "free breakfast" at level 6 every morning by just simply walk-in in the cafe and pretend they are staying in thst hotel (no staff check on the breakfast coupons). On top of that, breakfast was supposed to be at 6am, but most of the staffs came in only at 7am to prepare the foods and their excuse is "we are short of staffs, and sorry they might be late". Eventhough the hotel is attached to the "biggest mall" in Borneo, it still far from the city. Will take at least 20mins to get to the city. Traffic was horrible. You'll definitely need own transportation/car rental or call for taxi (which will cost you more money) to move around unless you are planning just only to shop at the mall. They gave us free internet but the it was too damn slow. My 3g tablet works better. Overall all I can say, this is 1 of the worst hotel I've had stayed. However there are few more hotels at the same building should you need to stay in 1 Borneo.

I questioned him why he allowed her to cut queue as we have been waiting before her. He was rude and replied that the female customer did not cut queue and continue to serve her and ignore us.

reviews.title: Poorly maintained rooms
reviews.text:
I stayed in this hotel 2 nights, but decided to upgrade after the first night. Apart from having a window which wasn't facing a wall and actually had sunlight shining in, the second room was just as badly maintained as the first.

Rooms were small, but that wasn't the issue. 
The rooms were badly maintained. Wallpapers were peeling all over, dust collected on the higher areas of the room (Ceiling/high windows) as though it was never cleaned for 10 years, room doors were not smooth and took some force to shut it every time (even the hydraulics were removed), there were a lot of ants running around on the table and in the toilet sink, some light switches did not work, I presumed either the bulbs were just not working or completely removed. This made the room very dark.

Other negatives: Pillows were too soft, toiletries were bare minimal, both rooms had problems with the shower stall where every time i took a shower, the whole bathroom floor will be flooded, leaking out of the sower stall. TV only had 4 local TV channels, 1 sports channel. Bed lamp shades were too dark, no WIFI except lobby.

All in all, for the price, it was not worth it. You would be better off staying at one of the other 3 hotels located at the same 1Borneo.

reviews.text:
I checked in with my father who's wheelchair bound with small kids.

1. The was no qualm with the facilities for the kids.  The saving grace is One Borneo where they could find plenty of distractions. 
2. My issues were facilities for disabled & lack of services served by the staffs:-
- The elevator isn't even (the gap betwen the floor and the lift is too big, causing multiple almost-accident incidents.  This is a high hazard environment.  This can actually be reported to DOSH.
-  Staffs were not friendly & had little knowledge of facilities for disabled that was really frustrating for me and the family.

Suggestions:-
1. Facilities for the disabled can be improved.
2. Staffs should have better knowledge of its facilities for disabled.
3. Upgrade the system.

reviews.title: Decent Hotel
reviews.text: This hotel is a 50RM cab ride from the airport. It's a fair distance from the older downtown area like Gaya ST or the waterfront. The hotel is 1 of 3 connected to the 1Borneo mall. This is advertised as Malyasia's biggest mall, but it is smaller than I expected. Pictures of the hotel are a lot nicer than the actual hotel itself. My first impression what that if I were paying more a night I'd be disappointed. Unless you plan on eating at the mall, then food options are limited; particularly after 9pm when the mall starts to shut down. The room was ok, but not as nice as I had expected. Wall paper literally being taped up in the corner, but covered by the curtains. Furniture is beat up. Room could use a remodel. Again, not terrible, but not like the pics. Not much to do in this part of town other than the mall. Next time I go to KK I'll stay back in the Gaya ST area by the waterfront.

reviews.title: Not worth of money.
reviews.text:
The 3-star rating is misleading and does not reflect the actual experience, which is closer to that of a 1-star establishment.

1. The parking situation is utterly frustrating. Guests are charged RM12 for parking, but if you leave, your spot is not guaranteed upon your return. If someone else takes your space, you will be forced to park in the mall's paid parking area, which incurs additional hourly or overnight charges. If you’re local then you well aware of the situation leaving your vehicle somewhere else.

2. The breakfast included with the room is extremely disappointing. The limited options consist of fried kuew tiaw, hash browns, corn, sausage, ikan masak sambal, one piece of fruit, and bubur kacang merah, with coffee and tea. If you want something as basic as an omelette or a lighter breakfast, you'll need to pay extra.

3. Surprisingly, even basic amenities are not complimentary. You are charged RM8 for just two servings each of coffee, tea, sugar, and creamer.

The service provided is far below acceptable standards, and even budget hotels in Tawau offer a better experience. Given the quality, the price should be well below RM100.

reviews.text: My dad and mum is the one that's staying here. But I did visit them at the hotel. The room assigned was so small, and what's worst is that there's a vertical beam right inside the room and it makes the access to the room. What I hate about the room is the wallpapers are very dirty and torn. The sink is cracked and it looks dirty. The good part of this hotel is all the basic necessity is provided: iron, coffee tea, tv, mini refrigerator, and internet (cabled). My parents says that the breakfast is nice. Lastly, its got easy access to car park, shopping complex  and Mount Kinabalu view.
